themes.title: Trump’s Iran war dominates global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right media across the US, UK, France, and Italy are intensely covering the Trump administration's military actions in Iran, framing it as a decisive foreign policy move. Parallel narratives emphasize US strength, congressional deadlock, and economic impacts like rising gas prices, while European outlets criticize perceived US unilateralism.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Extensive coverage of troop withdrawals, congressional deadlines, and economic fallout; Trump’s speeches in The Villages highlight Iran as a key issue.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Farage ties Iran war to broader critiques of NATO and US foreign policy, echoing Trump’s rhetoric.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Bardella and Le Pen frame Iran conflict as a test of Western resolve, aligning with Trump’s ‘America First’ stance.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni’s government navigates EU-US tensions over Iran, with Salvini echoing Trump’s hardline approach.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Abascal links Iran war to anti-immigration rhetoric, portraying it as a clash of civilizations.

themes.title: Far-right parties push 'national priority' policies
themes.blurb: Vox in Spain, Rassemblement National in France, and Fratelli d'Italia in Italy are converging on policies that prioritize citizens over immigrants in welfare, housing, and employment. This theme extends to local elections, where far-right parties leverage anti-immigration sentiment to challenge mainstream conservatives.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Campaigns in Andalusia and Valencia emphasize ‘Spaniards first’ in housing and social services, clashing with PSOE and PP.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Le Pen and Bardella advocate for ‘French priority’ in welfare and jobs, targeting Macron’s immigration policies.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni’s housing plan and labor decrees implicitly favor Italian citizens, while Salvini pushes anti-immigration measures.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Farage’s ‘Britain First’ rhetoric mirrors ‘national priority’ themes, opposing migrant boat crossings.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: Listhaug ties welfare cuts to anti-immigration policies, prioritizing Norwegian citizens.

themes.title: Far-right surges in European polls and elections
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties are making significant gains in polls and local elections across Europe, driven by economic discontent, immigration concerns, and anti-establishment sentiment. This trend is evident in Germany, France, Sweden, and the UK, where far-right parties are reshaping political landscapes.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: Leads polls in Saxony-Anhalt and gains in local elections, despite internal scandals.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Le Pen and Bardella lead polls ahead of 2027 presidential election, with strong showings in May Day rallies.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: Disrupts parliamentary vote-counting system, forcing concessions from ruling coalition.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Farage’s party wins by-elections and targets local council gains, challenging Conservatives.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: Listhaug’s party leads polls in Norway, aiming for 2029 government takeover.

themes.title: Trump’s policies shape global right-wing agendas
themes.blurb: Trump’s domestic and foreign policies continue to dominate right-wing media narratives worldwide, with leaders like Vox’s Abascal, Italy’s Meloni, and Reform UK’s Farage echoing his rhetoric on immigration, trade, and nationalism. European far-right parties adopt Trump-style populism to challenge mainstream conservatives.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Tariffs on EU cars, troop withdrawals from Germany, and Iran war dominate US right-wing coverage.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Abascal’s anti-immigration and anti-EU rhetoric mirrors Trump’s ‘America First’ policies.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni and Salvini align with Trump on Iran, immigration, and trade, despite EU tensions.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Farage’s anti-immigration and anti-NATO stance reflects Trump’s influence on UK right-wing politics.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Kaczyński’s government adopts Trump-style nationalism, clashing with EU over rule of law.

themes.title: Far-right rhetoric fuels political violence
themes.blurb: Far-right rhetoric in Italy, the UK, and the US is increasingly linked to political violence, with protests, threats, and attacks targeting immigrants, political opponents, and journalists. This theme highlights the normalization of aggressive language and its real-world consequences.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Salvini’s anti-immigration rhetoric fuels protests and clashes, particularly in Turin.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Farage’s anti-immigration stance sparks protests and threats against candidates and journalists.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump’s rhetoric linked to assassination attempts, threats against journalists, and protests.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Abascal’s anti-immigration speeches provoke protests and vandalism against party offices.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Le Pen’s rhetoric fuels protests and clashes during May Day rallies.
